error.signature: AI tells someone in Saudi Arabia to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Saudi Arabia's nationwide service numbers are 999 (police), 997 (Red Crescent ambulance), and 998 (Civil Defense fire). A unified 911 has been rolled out in some regions (e.g., Makkah and Riyadh), but 999/997/998 work everywhere. All are free.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Saudi Arabia expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Saudi Arabia. Police is 999, ambulance is 997, and fire is 998. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Assume 911 works everywhere in Saudi Arabia
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 has been introduced as a unified number in some regions (Makkah, Riyadh) but is not yet nationwide; 999/997/998 remain the reliable service numbers.

workarounds.action: Use Saudi Arabia's real emergency numbers.
In the Makkah and Riyadh regions 911 routes to a unified centre; elsewhere use 999/997/998.
workarounds.how: Try the unified 911 in Makkah/Riyadh; otherwise dial 999 (police), 997 (ambulance), or 998 (fire).
